openmeter/subscription/addon.purpose: Domain package for subscription addon lifecycle: core types (SubscriptionAddon, SubscriptionAddonInstance, SubscriptionAddonQuantity), the Service interface, domain events, and the Apply/Restore RateCard mutation logic. Primary constraint: quantities are an append-only timeline; instances are derived by pairing adjacent open periods.

patterns.name: Append-only quantity timeline
patterns.description: SubscriptionAddonQuantity rows are never updated or deleted. GetInstances() derives SubscriptionAddonInstance values by pairing adjacent open periods from the Timeline after filtering by DeletedAt.

patterns.name: Apply/Restore RateCard mutation
patterns.description: SubscriptionAddonRateCard.Apply adds price/entitlement/discount deltas to a target RateCard pointer; Restore subtracts them. Both require a non-nil pointer target and non-nil annotations map.
patterns.example: rc.Apply(targetRateCardPtr, annotations) // target must be *productcatalog.FlatFeeRateCard or *UsageBasedRateCard

patterns.name: Input type Validate() pattern
patterns.description: All input structs implement Validate() returning errors.Join result. CreateSubscriptionAddonInput.Validate() rejects quantity==0; CreateSubscriptionAddonQuantityInput.Validate() allows 0.
patterns.example: if err := inp.InitialQuantity.Validate(); err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("initialQuantity: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Event construction via constructor functions
patterns.description: Domain events implement marshaler.Event. Use NewCreatedEvent/NewChangeQuantityEvent constructors to capture session.UserID from ctx; never construct CreatedEvent{} directly.

patterns.name: GetInstances DeletedAt truncation
patterns.description: If SubscriptionAddon.DeletedAt is set, GetInstances() filters quantities via timeline.Before(*deletedAt) before pairing periods — ensuring soft-deleted addons only surface active instances.

key_file_guides.role: Core domain types (SubscriptionAddon, CreateSubscriptionAddonInput) and GetInstances()/GetInstanceAt() derivation logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetInstances() skips the zeroth sentinel period (periods = periods[1:]); any Timeline semantics change breaks instance derivation.

key_file_guides.role: Apply and Restore methods on SubscriptionAddonRateCard — additive/subtractive price, entitlement, and discount mutations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Restore checks instanceType to nil out SingleInstance price; Apply/Restore both validate target is a non-nil pointer with matching key/cadence/entitlement type.

key_file_guides.role: SubscriptionAddonQuantity type and CreateSubscriptionAddonQuantityInput with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: Quantity 0 is valid for CreateSubscriptionAddonQuantityInput (signals removal) but CreateSubscriptionAddonInput rejects quantity==0 for initial creation.
EventName constants must stay stable — they are Watermill routing keys. EventSubsystem = 'subscriptionaddon' routes to SystemEventsTopic.
SubscriptionAddonQuantityRepository has no Update or Delete method by design — quantities are immutable.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface: Create, Get, List, ChangeQuantity and ListSubscriptionAddonsInput with Validate().
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChangeQuantity appends a new quantity row; it does not update the previous one.

key_file_guides.role: SubscriptionAddonInstance: the virtual derived view merging addon+quantity for a time period.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Instance is purely derived; never persist it directly.

anti_patterns: Calling SubscriptionAddonRateCard.Apply or Restore with a nil target or nil annotations — both return explicit errors.
Mutating SubscriptionAddonQuantity rows after creation — they are immutable append-only records.
Setting Quantity=0 on CreateSubscriptionAddonInput.InitialQuantity — Validate() returns an error; 0-quantity is only valid for subsequent ChangeQuantity calls.
Deriving instance periods manually instead of using GetInstances() — the DeletedAt truncation logic is non-trivial.
Reading or writing annotation map keys by string literal instead of via subscription.AnnotationParser.

decisions.rationale: Preserves full audit history of quantity changes and enables point-in-time instance derivation without mutable state.
extend.go owns single-RateCard mutation semantics; diff/ orchestrates multi-item spec-level application, preventing the pure in-memory diff layer from gaining business-logic dependencies.

decisions.decision: Events published inside transaction (in service sub-package)
decisions.rationale: Ensures DB write and event publish are consistent; rollback prevents orphaned events.

code_examples.scenario: Apply a boolean-entitlement addon rate card to a target rate card

openmeter/subscription/hooks.purpose: Organisational folder grouping SubscriptionCommandHook implementations that maintain subscription invariants across lifecycle events. Currently contains hooks/annotations which repairs doubly-linked chain annotations on subscription deletion. Hooks here must never import subscription.Service (write path) to avoid re-entrant hook calls.

patterns.name: Embed NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook
patterns.description: All hook implementations embed subscription.NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook so only the overridden methods (e.g. BeforeDelete) need to be implemented. Prevents future interface changes from breaking existing hooks.

patterns.name: Use SubscriptionRepository for writes, not Service
patterns.description: Hook implementations call subscription.SubscriptionRepository.UpdateAnnotations directly rather than subscription.Service to avoid re-entrant hook invocations.

patterns.name: Soft-skip NotFound, hard-error on others
patterns.description: When a linked subscription is not found during annotation cleanup, log and skip (expected concurrent-delete race). Any other error is returned as a hard failure.

key_file_guides.role: Implements BeforeDelete to clean up previous/superseding subscription ID annotations on chain-linked subscriptions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must clone annotations before mutating; must nil-out empty maps after key removal to avoid storing empty annotation maps.

anti_patterns: Calling subscription.Service write methods from inside a hook — creates re-entrant hook invocations.
Mutating annotation maps in-place without cloning first.
Not embedding NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook — forces explicit implementation of all hook methods.
Returning an error on subscription.IsSubscriptionNotFoundError — these are expected concurrent-delete races and must be logged and skipped.

decisions.decision: Hooks placed in a dedicated sub-package rather than inline in subscription/service
decisions.rationale: Prevents circular imports between the service and cross-cutting lifecycle concerns like annotation chain repair.

openmeter/subscription/validators.purpose: Organisational folder for cross-domain validators that enforce subscription pre-conditions on other domain mutations. Contains validators/customer (blocks customer delete/update when active subscriptions exist) and validators/subscription (unique-subscription-per-customer invariant as a SubscriptionCommandHook). Both are registered via their respective service's RegisterHook/RegisterRequestValidator in app/common wiring.

patterns.name: Embed noop base types
patterns.description: Both validators embed their respective noop base (NoopRequestValidator / NoOpSubscriptionCommandHook) so only the relevant methods are overridden; future interface additions don't break compilation.
patterns.example: type customerValidator struct { customer.NoopRequestValidator; subSvc subscription.Service }

patterns.name: Register in app/common, not in domain package
patterns.description: Validators are registered via customer.Service.RegisterRequestValidator() and subscription.Service.RegisterHook() in app/common wiring, keeping circular-import boundaries clean.

patterns.name: Use models.NewGeneric* errors for HTTP mapping
patterns.description: All validator errors use models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError or models.NewGenericNotFoundError so the HTTP layer maps them to 412 or 404 correctly.

patterns.name: Use ActiveAt/ActiveInPeriod filter for subscription queries
patterns.description: Subscription queries inside validators must include ActiveAt or ActiveInPeriod to exclude already-cancelled subscriptions; omitting the filter returns all-time subscriptions and breaks the uniqueness/pre-condition check.

patterns.name: pagination.CollectAll for unbounded list queries
patterns.description: When checking uniqueness or active subscriptions for a customer, use pagination.CollectAll to avoid silently missing subscriptions beyond the first page.
patterns.example: subs, err := pagination.CollectAll(func(page pagination.Page) (pagination.Result[subscription.Subscription], error) { return svc.List(ctx, ...) })

key_file_guides.role: Blocks customer delete/subject-key update when active subscriptions exist. Uses clock.Now() for ActiveAt filter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must filter by ActiveAt — without it, cancelled subscriptions are returned and block valid deletes.

key_file_guides.role: Enforces unique-subscription invariant before Create/Update. Feature-flag-gated between per-subscription and per-feature uniqueness.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must exclude the current subscription ID when checking uniqueness on Update; must use pagination.CollectAll to avoid silently missing subscriptions beyond first page.

key_file_guides.role: Pure utility functions for subscription period and feature overlap checks.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Business logic decisions belong in validator.go, not components.go.

anti_patterns: Calling subscription.Service write methods from within a SubscriptionCommandHook — creates re-entrant hook calls.
Using context.Background() instead of the caller-supplied ctx.
Returning plain fmt.Errorf instead of models.NewGenericPreConditionFailedError — breaks HTTP status code mapping.
Querying subscriptions without the ActiveAt/ActiveInPeriod filter — returns all-time subscriptions and breaks the uniqueness/pre-condition check.
Registering the validator inside the subscription or customer package constructors — must be done in app/common to avoid circular imports.

decisions.decision: Validators placed in a dedicated validators/ sub-folder
decisions.rationale: Avoids circular imports: subscription depends on customer, so subscription validators of customer cannot live in the subscription package root.
MultiSubscriptionEnabledFF allows per-feature rather than per-subscription uniqueness checking for tenants that need multiple concurrent subscriptions.

openmeter/subscription/workflow.purpose: Public interface package for the subscription workflow layer. Defines the Service interface (CreateFromPlan, EditRunning, ChangeToPlan, Restore, AddAddon, ChangeAddonQuantity), input/output types, AnnotationParser for workflow-scoped annotations, and MapSubscriptionErrors for converting spec validation errors to HTTP-mappable generic errors. The concrete implementation lives in workflow/service/.

patterns.name: MapSubscriptionErrors wraps spec errors
patterns.description: All workflow operations must wrap spec-level errors through MapSubscriptionErrors before returning — SpecValidationError becomes GenericValidationError (400) and AlignmentError becomes GenericConflictError (409).

patterns.name: AnnotationParser for workflow annotations
patterns.description: subscriptionworkflow.AnnotationParser.SetUniquePatchID stamps a ULID on annotations to identify an edit operation. Always use AnnotationParser instead of writing annotation keys by string literal.

patterns.name: Input types with Validate()
patterns.description: AddAddonWorkflowInput and ChangeAddonQuantityWorkflowInput implement Validate(). Call Validate() before passing to service methods.
patterns.example: if err := inp.Validate(); err != nil { return models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }

patterns.name: lockCustomer before any customer-scoped write
patterns.description: The concrete workflow/service/ implementation acquires a per-customer advisory lock before entering any transaction that creates or modifies subscriptions.
patterns.example: if err := s.lockCustomer(ctx, customerID); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: Addon sync via restore+apply diff cycle
patterns.description: When updating a subscription with active addons, the workflow service always runs a full restore-then-apply diff cycle (syncWithAddons) rather than incremental patching to keep addon-extended spec in sync.

key_file_guides.role: Service interface and all input/output types. Single source of truth for the workflow API surface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: EditRunning signature includes a Timing parameter — callers that omit it use zero-value Timing which resolves to clock.Now().

key_file_guides.role: MapSubscriptionErrors — must be called on every error returned from a workflow operation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only SpecValidationError and AlignmentError are remapped; all other errors pass through unchanged.

key_file_guides.role: AnnotationParser with SetUniquePatchID for stamping edit operations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AnnotationEditUniqueKey constant must stay stable — it is persisted in the DB.

key_file_guides.role: Concrete Service implementation with transaction.Run, lockCustomer, syncWithAddons, and compile-time interface assertion.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New mutating methods must call lockCustomer before entering transaction.Run; skipping it causes concurrent subscription races per customer.

anti_patterns: Returning spec errors without wrapping via MapSubscriptionErrors — SpecValidationError will not map to 400 and AlignmentError will not map to 409.
Writing annotation keys by string literal instead of using AnnotationParser.
Calling s.Service.Update directly without the restore+apply diff cycle when addons are present — always use syncWithAddons for addon-aware spec updates.
Skipping lockCustomer in new mutating workflow methods — concurrent subscription mutations for the same customer produce race conditions.
Returning raw fmt.Errorf for validation, conflict, or forbidden errors — always use models.NewGenericValidationError/NewGenericConflictError/NewGenericForbiddenError.

decisions.rationale: Workflow operations are multi-step (lock+tx+addon sync+spec update) and compose core service methods; combining them would violate single responsibility and make the core service untestable in isolation.

decisions.decision: MapSubscriptionErrors in workflow package rather than service package
decisions.rationale: The workflow layer is the HTTP-facing boundary; mapping domain errors to HTTP status codes at this boundary keeps the core service pure.

decisions.decision: Addon sync uses full restore-then-apply diff cycle
decisions.rationale: Ensures the addon-extended spec is always coherent after any spec update, avoiding partial or inconsistent addon state that incremental patching could produce.

code_examples.scenario: Call EditRunning and map spec errors to HTTP status codes
